data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (criterion T1 — Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay]

T1 bright-line FIRES (a novel architectural pattern: a plan-review verification gate firing at open→in_progress). The overlay therefore applies, and the plan handles all three T1 checks well overall.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria passed):
- T1(a) prior-art considered / anti-reinvention: STRONG. The whole design is framed as the *inverse of an existing rebar feature* (the completion-verifier close gate) and is built ON four named prior epics rather than reinvented — three-pass framework `9da1`, workflow engine `e6d9`, prompt storage `da27`, grounding oracle `8f6c`. Layer-1 explicitly "does NOT add its own tool integrations… it CONSUMES the shipped oracle." Coaching/verdict mechanisms cite external prior art (Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE, RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK; lost-in-the-middle; anti-sycophancy; "external-grounded > self-reflection") plus ~30 research reports. This is the opposite of repackaging-without-consideration.
- T1(c) unverified capability assertions resolved: STRONG. The load-bearing "X is shipped, consume it" claims are GROUNDED — the `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan` oracle facade exists at src/rebar/grounding/ (verified: src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py, oracle.py, resolve.py) and is already consumed by src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py. The cap=10 / chunking / tier defaults are honestly labeled "experiment-informed but INCONCLUSIVE ALONE / tune from dogfood data," not asserted as validated constants — the right posture for an unproven assertion.

The single finding below is minor coaching, not a blocker.

- [ ] State the prior-art / "why not an off-the-shelf agentic-eval framework" rationale for the gate's own novel three-pass + Pass-4-coach pipeline as explicitly as it is stated for the consumed prerequisites. (criteria: T1; T1(b) novelty-justification. location: "Design summary" / "Review structure — three-pass" sections.) The plan justifies the INVERSE-of-close-gate framing and grounds the verdict MECHANISM in research, but the higher-level architectural choice — a bespoke 4-pass (find→verify→deterministic-gate→coach) review pipeline rather than an existing agentic-eval/LLM-judge harness (e.g. the Inspect-AI / promptfoo seams the plan already wires into, or G-Eval/LLM-as-judge rubric runners) — is asserted via the `9da1` adoption rather than weighed against those alternatives. evidence: the plan adopts the three-pass design "from epic 9da1" and the Pass-4 coach is novel-by-construction (locked move-registry templates), but no section says "we considered an off-the-shelf judge/eval pipeline and chose a custom one because…". This is a Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging gap only at the pipeline-shape level; the per-pass mechanisms are well-grounded. suggested_fix: (empty — defer to author; the rationale likely lives in session log 63cc but isn't surfaced in this self-contained epic summary.)
